name: Q1891968
abstract: Isham "Manzie" Johnson (August 19, 1906 – April 9, 1971) was an American jazz drummer. Johnson was raised in New York City, and played in Harlem in the 1920s with Fats Waller, James P.

about_quotes.speaker: Quentin Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: Manzie Johnson was the drummer... We used to broadcast over CBS... we had -- Claude had left the band... Manzie Johnson and --

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: There was a drummer named Manzie Johnson... He and he played with was a drummer in this particular band, Fats Waller.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Cully, Jack Butler and myself on trumpets... and Manzie Johnson, drums.

about_quotes.speaker: Jonah Jones
about_quotes.evidence: He had Frankie Newton and Manzie Johnson and some of the guys. So about -- and he started rehearsing them.

about_quotes.speaker: Theodore Leroy Bunn
about_quotes.evidence: in this particular session you were with Manzie Johnson. ... I know he's a good musician.
